an5.38:1.1: “These are the five rewards of conviction in a lay person.
an5.38:1.3: “When the truly good people in the world show compassion, they will first show compassion to people of conviction, and not to people without conviction.
an5.38:1.4: When visiting, they first visit people of conviction, and not people without conviction.
an5.38:1.5: When accepting gifts, they will first accept those from people with conviction, and not from people without conviction.
an5.38:1.6: When teaching the Dhamma, they will first teach those with conviction, and not those without conviction.
an5.38:1.7: A person of conviction, on the break-up of the body, after death, will arise in a good destination, a heavenly world.
an5.38:1.8: These are the five rewards of conviction in a lay person.“
an5.38:2.1: Just as a large banyan tree, on level ground where four roads meet, is a haven for the birds all around,
an5.38:2.2: even so a lay person of conviction is a haven for many people: monks, nuns, male lay followers, & female lay followers.”
an5.38:3.1: A massive tree whose branches carry fruits & leaves,
an5.38:4.3: Those seeking shade come to the shade,
an5.38:4.4: those seeking fruit find fruit to eat.
an5.38:5.4: To him come those without effluent—
an5.38:6.1: free from passion, free from aversion,
an5.38:6.3: the field of merit for the world.
an5.38:7.4: he is freed from effluents, totally unbound.
